column2: Any mode of First Person Camera - Continued

CameraMotion: Valve-style blur calculated from camera rotation and movement.
LocalBlur: Simple blur along local velocity vectors.
Reconstruction: Performs plausible motion blur using tile-based velocity estimation.
ReconstructionDX11: Higher-quality version optimized for DirectX 11.
ReconstructionDisc: Uses disc-shaped kernel sampling for performance.
column3: Keep the algorithm names (such as CameraMotion) unchanged. You may append the translation in parentheses after the name, e.g. 'CameraMotion (YOUR TRANSLATION)'.

column2: This setting only applies when "{0}" is set to CameraMotion.

Controls how much camera translation contributes to motion blur.
Higher values result in more noticeable blur when the camera moves.

Controls how much camera rotation contributes to motion blur.
Higher values result in stronger blur when turning.

Sets the maximum length of motion blur trails.
Lower values produce a tighter blur; higher values allow longer streaks.
For Reconstruction-based filters, this value is capped at 10.

Sets the minimum speed below which no blur is applied.
Lower values cause even subtle motion to be blurred.

Global multiplier applied to all velocity vectors.
Increase to exaggerate motion blur intensity.

Adjusts how depth differences influence blur transitions.
Lower values create sharper separation between foreground and background; higher values blend them smoothly.

The amount of random offset applied to sampling positions, which helps reduce banding artifacts.
Higher values produce softer blur but may introduce visible noise.
Works in all modes. However, when switching to a mode metioned above, the state will be overridden by its settings.
